images.caption: Figure 1:Geospatial context of the study domain. (a) Location of IMIP (red circle) within the Indonesian archipelago; the dashed rectangle delineates the study area. (b) Tolo Bay and the western Banda Sea at 15 arc-second bathymetric resolution (SRTM15+V2), showing the impact zone (red box) directly offshore of IMIP and the control zone (blue box) in the open Banda Sea. The color scale denotes elevation and bathymetry in meters.

Figure 2:Annual LULC maps of the IMIP impact zone derived from Sentinel-2 at 10 m resolution for (a) 2017 through (h) 2024. Eight classes are shown: water (blue), trees (dark green), flooded vegetation (light green), crops (orange), built area (red), bare ground (tan), clouds (gray), and rangeland (yellow-green).

Figure 3:Three-level intensity analysis of LULC transitions in the IMIP impact zone, 2017–2024. (a) Interval-level: annual change intensityStS_{t}for each year-pair; the dashed line marksUintU_{\mathrm{int}}. (b) Category-level: gain (right) and loss (left) intensities; dashed lines markUcatU_{\mathrm{cat}}. (c) Transition-level: selected intensities for built area gain sources and tree cover loss sinks; dashed lines mark the uniform thresholds. Red bars denote active/targeted transitions; gray bars denote dormant/avoided transitions.

Figure 4:Monthly climatology of the entire study area (1998–2024) for (a)Kd​(490)K_{d}(490)(×10−2\times 10^{-2}m-1), (b) SST (∘C), and (c) SSS (PSU). Points and error bars show the median and 95% bootstrap confidence interval (B=10,000B=10{,}000); shading denotes the IQR.

Figure 5:MonthlyKd​(490)K_{d}(490)(×10−2\times 10^{-2}m-1) for the impact zone (red) and control zone (blue), January 1998 to December 2024. The orange dashed line marks first smelter commissioning (April 2015); the purple dash-dotted line marks the export ban (January 2020).

Figure 6:Changepoint model selection for the impact zone (top) and control zone (bottom). (a,c) Number of breakpoints versus penalty parameter; the red dashed line marks the optimal penalty. (b,d) BIC versuskk; the red dashed line marks the BIC-optimalkk.

Figure 7:Consensus regime segmentation ofKd​(490)K_{d}(490)for (a) the control zone and (b) the impact zone. Horizontal lines and shading indicate regime means±1​σ\pm 1\sigma. In (a), no consensus breakpoint was detected (single regime,μ=3.79\mu=3.79,σ=0.50\sigma=0.50). In (b), a consensus breakpoint at May 2019 (p<0.001p<0.001) separates Regime 1 (μ=4.32\mu=4.32,σ=0.45\sigma=0.45) from Regime 2 (μ=5.38\mu=5.38,σ=0.77\sigma=0.77).

Figure 8:BSTS causal impact analysis with intervention at May 2019. (a) ObservedKd​(490)K_{d}(490)(black) and counterfactual (blue) with 95% credible interval. (b) Pointwise causal effectδt\delta_{t}with 95% interval. (c) Cumulative effectΔT\Delta_{T}; red shading indicates positive accumulation, blue negative. The red dashed line marks the intervention.
